description: Fix plugin list offline display issues found during fresh-user testing. When the MCP server is not running, `opentabs plugin list` shows raw npm package names (e.g., @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack) instead of human-friendly display names (e.g., Slack), and shows toolCount: 0 in JSON output even though the plugin's tool manifest is readable from disk.

userStories.title: Show display names and tool counts for npm plugins in offline mode
userStories.description: As a user with the server stopped, I want `opentabs plugin list` to show the human-friendly display name (e.g., 'Slack') and accurate tool count for npm-installed plugins, so that the output is consistent with what I see when the server is running.
acceptanceCriteria: With a plugin like @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack installed globally and the server stopped, `opentabs plugin list` shows 'Slack' as the display name (not '@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack')
With the server stopped, `opentabs plugin list` shows the correct tool count (e.g., '22 tools') for npm plugins that have a built dist/tools.json
With the server stopped, `opentabs plugin list --json` shows the correct displayName from the plugin's package.json opentabs.displayName field and accurate toolCount from dist/tools.json
With the server stopped, `opentabs plugin list --verbose` shows tool names read from dist/tools.json
If a plugin has no dist/tools.json, the tool count gracefully falls back to 0 and verbose shows no tools
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts

The issue is in `scanNpmPlugins()` (line 510). It currently reads `npm list -g --json` which only gives package names and versions — no display names or tool counts. The entries it creates (lines 524-531) have `displayName: pkgName` (the raw npm name) and `toolCount: 0`.

The fix: after scanning npm packages, resolve each plugin's install directory and read its package.json + dist/tools.json to get the real displayName and tool count. The function `readLocalPluginInfo()` (line 467) already does exactly this — it reads package.json for opentabs.displayName and dist/tools.json for tool names/counts.

Approach:
1. In `scanNpmPlugins()`, after getting the list of plugin names from npm list, determine the global root directory (already shown how at line 119: `npm root -g`)
2. For each plugin found, call `readLocalPluginInfo(join(globalRoot, pkgName))` to get display name, tool count, and tool names
3. Use those values instead of the hardcoded defaults

Alternatively, enhance `scanNpmPlugins()` to accept a `globalRoot` parameter and read the info inline, avoiding the extra npm call.

Existing tests: platform/cli/src/commands/__tests__/plugin.test.ts — add test cases for offline display.
